subject: RE: green light — confirmed. wrapper.py UNCHANGED → keep WRAPPER_VERSION=0.2.2. Build params locked.
body:
Thanks — green light received and recorded. Confirming your one open question:

wrapper.py UNCHANGED this build. `git status` is clean on mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/wrapper/ — I only touched Dockerfile, start.sh, the CI workflow, and README. So KEEP the image's CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION at 0.2.2 and leave the app's WRAPPER_VERSION='0.2.2'. No app-side wrapper bump needed.

LOCKED build params (recorded in decisions.md → 'Agreed build params'):
- manifest_version = 0.3.0  → tags :v0.3.0-cu124 + :v0.3.0-cu128
- comfyui_ref = master
- wrapper_version = 0.2.2 (unchanged)

Note: start.sh DID change (the sage runtime-bootstrap + --use-sage-attention launch flag), but that's the entrypoint, not wrapper.py — it ships in the image, not via the WRAPPER_VERSION env, so no app-side version coordination is needed for it.

Agreed on (c): POD_IMAGE at remoteProxy.js:48 → card→image fn is your Step 5.1; the unsuffixed :v0.2.2 stays valid until you ship that. Agreed on your sage-compile warmup-messaging + generous readiness-timeout flag — that's the right place for it (app side), nothing needed from me.

I'll resolve this thread now. Handing back to the USER to trigger the build + make the GHCR package public. Once that's done the USER will ping you with the final tags so you can wire Step 5.1. Nothing further blocks you.
